findings.description: reachable() never checks seen before enqueuing neighbors, so on a cyclic graph (which the docstring explicitly supports) the queue grows unbounded and the BFS never terminates.
reachable() adds neighbors to seen but never includes start, so a node reachable only via a cycle back to start is inconsistent and start itself is excluded from its own reachable set.
Iterating self._edges[node] on a defaultdict inserts empty lists for every leaf node, mutating the graph as a side effect of a read-only traversal.
count_paths() has no memoization, so a DAG with diamond/shared subpaths causes exponential recomputation of the same (neighbor, dst) subproblems.
count_paths() recurses with no cycle guard, so on a cyclic graph (acyclicity is only assumed, never enforced) it infinitely recurses into RecursionError.
count_paths() uses unbounded Python recursion and will raise RecursionError on graphs deeper than the interpreter recursion limit even when acyclic.

topological_order returns None to signal a cycle but the Optional return type forces every caller to disambiguate cycle-vs-empty-graph, and an empty graph returns [] which is indistinguishable from a trivially-ordered one.
Iterable is imported from typing but never used.
PEP 585 builtin generics (dict[str, list[str]], set[str]) require Python 3.9+ with no __future__ import or version guard documented.
add_edge permits duplicate parallel edges, which inflates count_paths and indegree counts since neither dedupes the adjacency list.
